market_summary: REGIME: RISK-ON INFLECTION — SPY close=679.91, now ABOVE BOTH MA50=673.27 and MA200=660.58 (first dual-MA reclaim in the run series after 7+ sessions below); QQQ also above both MAs (1m=+0.53%, above MA50=600.52 and MA200=594.96); IWM above both MAs with 1m=+3.58% — small-cap breadth confirming. TLT below MA50=87.39 but above MA200=86.60 (close=86.70) — no bond flight-to-safety bid but no panic either; rates pressure easing marginally. BTC-USD mom=-10.75%, 3m=+14.46%, above MA50 but below MA200=$88,479 — crypto partial recovery but not investable. GLD close=437.91, above MA200=381.40 with +14.8% buffer, 1m=-8.36% — structural inflation hedge intact despite near-term pullback from highs; still below MA50=452.33. Portfolio weighted avg momentum ~+34% vs SPY mom=+9.52% — approximately +25pp alpha spread maintained. Regime UPGRADE to MIXED/STABILIZING; risk-on signals are real but tariff/macro uncertainty warrants continued defensive positioning.

LEADERSHIP CONFIRMED — All 8 holdings retain positive momentum and MA200 support. KLAC leads universe at mom=73.41%, 1m=+18.88%, 3m=+27.20%, ABOVE BOTH MAs (close=1727.26 vs MA50=1480.88) — exceptional quality+momentum: Q2-FY2026 EPS=$8.73 (beat), Q3 est=$9.74, Q4=$12.10 (+39% ramp from Q2); OM=42.4%, ROE/ROIC elite. TSM recovers to above BOTH MAs (close=365.49 vs MA50=349.35) with mom=49.69%, 1m=+5.60%, 3m=+15.01% — tariff binary easing. XOM mom=32.49%, ABOVE BOTH MAs, EPS Q2-est=$2.35, FCF yield solid; CVX mom=23.36%, ABOVE BOTH MAs, Q2-est EPS=$2.46. LMT mom=23.61%, ABOVE MA200=511.56 but still BELOW MA50=636.13 (close=623.87, gap=-1.9%) — monitoring flag persists; Q2-est EPS=$7.28, FCF trajectory intact. AVGO mom=36.35%, ABOVE BOTH MAs (close=354.91 vs MA200=328.39) — 1w=+13.21%, strong confirmation of yesterday's add. COST mom=11.99%, ABOVE BOTH MAs, vol20=13.44% — lowest volatility holding, defensive anchor confirmed. GLD structural thesis intact.

GO/NO-GO: NON-REBALANCE DAY — Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 1 (last checkpoint 2026-04-09; minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible window ~2026-04-16). Key positive developments from pack: (1) SPY and QQQ have both reclaimed MA50 and MA200 — regime upgrade signal; (2) TSM has recovered above MA50 — tariff-risk thesis partially resolved; (3) AVGO add from yesterday confirmed by 1w=+13.21% bounce above both MAs; (4) KLAC 1m=+18.88% — highest momentum equity in universe. No kill-switch thresholds breached. Pre-logging for next rebalance (~2026-04-16): evaluate whether to reduce GLD allocation given SPY regime upgrade (GLD below MA50, structural thesis intact but risk-on rotation may reduce haven demand); monitor LMT MA50 recovery (close=623.87 vs MA50=636.13, -1.9% gap, still not healed); TSM above both MAs — consider size increase from 8% if tariff resolution continues.

Q2-FY2026 EPS est=$7.28 intact but Q2-2025 actual EPS=1.46 (a one-time write-down quarter) may create noisy comps. Defense budget uncertainty from DOGE program reviews is an unresolved idiosyncratic risk. No decisive thesis damage yet (above MA200=511.56 with +22% buffer, 3m=+26.22%), but monitoring flag remains active.

GLD close=437.91 below MA50=452.33 (gap=-3.2%) with 1m=-8.36% — near-term momentum impaired. SPY regime upgrade to above both MAs could trigger risk-on rotation away from haven assets, reducing structural demand for gold. However, MA200 buffer=+14.8% and 12m=+60.0% structural thesis intact. Not a full damage flag but a sizing watchpoint for next rebalance.

thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Policy mandates no portfolio changes. Days since last checkpoint = 1 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible window = 2026-04-16). Portfolio is well-positioned for the regime upgrade: SPY and QQQ both reclaimed MA50 and MA200 simultaneously — a significant bullish signal after 7+ sessions below. All 8 holdings retain positive momentum composites and MA200 support.

FACTOR EDGE REVIEW: Portfolio weighted momentum ~+34% vs SPY=+9.52% (+25pp spread). KLAC leads at mom=73.41% with confirmed EPS ramp ($8.73 Q2-FY2026 actual → $9.74 Q3 est → $12.10 Q4 est = +39% sequential ramp). TSM now above both MAs (tariff resolution signal). AVGO 1w=+13.21% validates yesterday's add. XOM/CVX energy pair maintaining defensive ballast. COST vol20=13.44% lowest in portfolio, drawdown tightest at dd63=-5.18%.

VARIANT VIEW: The regime upgrade from risk-off to mixed/stabilizing is the key development today. The portfolio's defensive tilt (40% Energy + 22% Commodities) may be slightly over-weighted for a risk-on environment, but the 3m momentum profiles of XOM (+31.72%) and CVX (+23.85%) remain superior to most risk-on alternatives. Pre-log for 2026-04-16 rebalance: evaluate modest reallocation from GLD (below MA50, risk-on rotation risk) toward TSM or AVGO size increases.
risks: MACRO: SPY regime upgrade may be a false breakout — tariff uncertainty remains elevated and a single negative trade headline could reverse the MA reclaim. GLD below MA50 signals real gold demand softness; if risk-on rotation accelerates, 22% GLD weight may create drag. Oil demand destruction risk if tariff-driven slowdown deepens — XOM/CVX 40% combined weight is the single largest concentration.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: LMT below MA50 for recurring sessions — if defense budget or F-35 program cuts materialize before next rebalance, 10% LMT weight creates meaningful downside. KLAC vol20=51.56% is elevated; a -10% adverse day would cost ~120bps at current 12% weight. AVGO (2%) is newly added and 1w=+13.21% bounce could mean short-term mean reversion before next rebalance window.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: No trades today — zero execution risk. Next rebalance window 2026-04-16: pre-flagging that potential GLD→TSM reallocation would involve moderately liquid instruments with estimated 10-15bps round-trip slippage each, well within cost hurdle given momentum spread.
falsifiable_checks: Portfolio alpha spread validation: weighted momentum spread vs SPY must remain above +20pp at next rebalance checkpoint (2026-04-16); current spread ~+25pp provides buffer but deterioration below +20pp would signal factor rotation requiring defensive repositioning.

LMT MA50 recovery check: LMT must close above MA50=636.13 at least twice in the next 5 sessions (by 2026-04-16) to confirm thesis recovery; continued failure to reclaim MA50 triggers evaluation of reduction from 10% to 6% with reallocation to TSM.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case (65%): Regime upgrade is real — SPY/QQQ dual-MA reclaim is technically significant after extended risk-off period. Portfolio is correctly positioned with high-momentum leaders (KLAC, TSM, AVGO) plus defensive Energy/Commodities ballast. No action needed today per policy; the portfolio as constructed benefits from both the momentum tailwind and defensive hedging. Bull case (25%): Tariff de-escalation continues, TSM above both MAs validates supply chain normalization, KLAC semiconductor cycle acceleration drives re-rating; GLD corrects modestly as haven demand fades. Bear case (10%): SPY MA reclaim is a false breakout, tariffs re-escalate, energy demand falls, and KLAC/TSM give back recent gains in a risk-off reversal. In bear case, GLD structural thesis provides portfolio ballast. Doing nothing today preserves optionality for 2026-04-16 rebalance when regime signal will have had more time to confirm.
